primary.title: EMI Calculator - Free Online Loan Payment Calculator
primary.description: Calculate monthly loan payments (EMI), total interest payable, and total repayment value with our free client-side calculator.
primary.heading: EMI Calculator
primary.seo_content:
Our EMI Calculator estimates monthly payments and total interest schedules. Here is the breakdown:

  • 1. Monthly Installment (EMI)

    Formula: EMI = [P × r × (1 + r)^n] / [(1 + r)^n - 1]
    Where: P = Principal, r = Monthly rate (Annual / 12 / 100), n = Tenure in months.
    Example: Borrowing $10,000 for 3 years at 10% interest rate results in an EMI of $322.67/month.

  • 2. Total Interest & Payback

    Formulas: Total Payback = EMI × n | Total Interest = Total Payback - P
    Example: For the $10,000 loan above, total payback is $11,616.12, carrying $1,616.12 in interest.

examples.title: Personal loan calculation
examples.description: If you take a personal loan of $10,000 for 3 years at a 10% interest rate, your monthly EMI will be approximately $322.67, with a total interest of $1,616.19.

examples.title: Short term business credit line
examples.description: For a business credit line of $50,000 at 8.5% interest rate over 5 years, the monthly payment will be $1,025.83.

faqs.question: What is the formula to calculate loan EMI?
faqs.answer: The mathematical formula for monthly EMI is: EMI = [P × r × (1 + r)^n] / [(1 + r)^n - 1], where P is the Principal loan amount, r is the monthly interest rate (annual rate / 12 / 100), and n is the loan tenure in months.

faqs.question: How can I reduce my total interest payable?
faqs.answer: You can reduce total interest payable by choosing a shorter loan tenure, making regular prepayments toward the principal, or negotiating a lower interest rate with your lender.

home-loan-calculator.title: Home Loan EMI Calculator - Estimate Mortgage Payments
home-loan-calculator.description: Plan your home purchase with our Home Loan EMI Calculator. Find monthly mortgage installments, total interest, and amortization details.
home-loan-calculator.heading: Home Loan EMI Calculator
home-loan-calculator.seo_content:
Plan your home purchase by estimating mortgage installments. Here is a standard calculation scenario:

  • Home Mortgage calculation

    Example: A home loan of $300,000 at 7% annual interest over 30 years results in a monthly payment of $1,995.91, with total interest of $418,527.00.

examples.title: Standard home purchase
examples.description: A home loan of $300,000 at 7% annual interest over 30 years results in a monthly payment of $1,995.91, with total interest of $418,527.

examples.title: 15-year accelerated home loan
examples.description: Borrowing $200,000 at 6.5% interest over 15 years yields a monthly installment of $1,742.22, saving substantial interest compared to a 30-year option.

faqs.question: What is the difference between home loan EMI and other EMIs?
faqs.answer: Home loans typically feature much larger principal amounts and longer tenures (up to 30 years), meaning interest costs represent a larger fraction of total repayment.

faqs.question: Does my home loan EMI include property tax or insurance?
faqs.answer: No, this calculator processes the base principal and interest loan repayment. Local property taxes and homeowner insurance premiums are usually managed separately.

car-loan-calculator.title: Car Loan EMI Calculator - Find Auto Loan Payments
car-loan-calculator.description: Calculate monthly car loan payments, interest charges, and total cost of ownership. Free auto installment tool.
car-loan-calculator.heading: Car Loan EMI Calculator
car-loan-calculator.seo_content:
A Car Loan EMI Calculator helps you compute the monthly installment needed to buy a new or used vehicle. Here is a standard auto finance example:

  • Car Loan calculation

    Example: A car loan of $25,000 at a 5% interest rate over 5 years results in a monthly payment of $471.78, with a total interest cost of $3,306.85.

examples.title: New sedan auto loan
examples.description: A car loan of $25,000 at a 5% interest rate over 5 years results in a monthly payment of $471.78, with a total interest cost of $3,306.85.

examples.title: Used SUV auto loan
examples.description: For a used vehicle loan of $15,000 at 7.5% interest rate over 4 years, the monthly payment will be $362.68.

faqs.question: What is a good tenure for a car loan?
faqs.answer: A shorter tenure (e.g. 3 to 5 years) reduces interest costs, while a longer tenure (6 to 7 years) lowers the monthly payment but increases total interest paid.

faqs.question: Should I make a down payment on my car loan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a larger down payment reduces the required loan principal, leading to smaller monthly payments and lower interest expense.
